Proposition 10.103. Let G be a group with augmentation ideal G. As an abelian group,G is free abelian with basis $G-1=\{x-1:x\in G,x\neq1\}$

We begin by examining homology groups.
Proposition 10.104. If A is a G-module, then
H_{0}(G,A)=\mathbb{Z}\otimes_{G}A\cong A/\mathcal{G}A.
Proof.By definition,$H_{0}(G,A)=\mathrm{T o r}_{0}^{\mathbb{Z}G}(\mathbb{Z},A)=\mathbb{Z}\otimes_{G}$ A. Applying the right exact functor $\otimes_{G}A$ to the exact sequence
0\to{\mathcal{G}}\to\mathbb{Z}G\to\mathbb{Z}\to0
gives exactness of the first row of the following commutative diagram:

\begin{array}{c}\mathcal{G}\otimes_{G}A\xrightarrow{\quad\quad}\mathbb{Z}G\otimes_{G}A\xrightarrow{\quad\quad}\mathbb{Z}\otimes_{G}A\xrightarrow{\quad\quad}0\\\big\downarrow\\\mathcal{G}A\xrightarrow{\quad\quad\quad}A\xrightarrow{\quad\quad\quad}A/\mathcal{G}A\xrightarrow{\quad\quad}0\end{array}
The two solid vertical arrows are given by t $u\otimes a\mapsto u a$ . By Proposition 8.93, there is an isomorphism $\mathbb{Z}\otimes_{G}A\cong A/\mathcal{G}A$
